Dunkirk (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $190,068,280Details
International Box Office $338,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $528,068,280
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $13,248,874
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $21,791,596
Total $35,040,470
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Hundreds of thousands of British and Allied troops are surrounded by enemy forces. Trapped on the beach with their backs to the sea they face an impossible situation as the enemy closes in.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$50,513,488 (26.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.76 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:36.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$150,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,720 opening theaters/4,014 max. theaters, 7.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $253,389,631

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 21st, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
July 21st, 2017 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
December 1st, 2017 (Oscar Qualifying Run) by Warner Bros.
April 11th, 2018 (Limited) by Warner Bros., released as Dunkirk (2017) (Re-Release)
International Releases: July 18th, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
July 19th, 2017 (Wide) (Sweden)
July 21st, 2017 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 21st, 2017 (Wide) (Poland)
July 21st, 2017 (Wide), released as Дюнкерк (Bulgaria)
Video Release: December 19th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense war experience and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2467 (Cert #50964), 3/15/2017)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: France, Germany
Plot point: Mass Evacuation, Nazis, War
Social setting: World War II
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Time period setting: 1940s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Syncopy, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment
Production Countries: United States, France, United Kingdom, Netherlands
Languages: English

Synopsis

Hundreds of thousands of British and Allied troops are surrounded by enemy forces. Trapped on the beach with their backs to the sea they face an impossible situation as the enemy closes in.
